---
title: "Supplementary material: Coastal-bird responses through Pacific herring spawning in the Strait of Georgia"
author:
  - name: "Jacob T. Dingwall"
date: ""
bibliography: mer_references_v7.bib
format:
  docx:
    reference-doc: mer_v7_times_new_roman_reference.docx
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: true
  html:
    css: mer_v7_print.css
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: true
execute:
  enabled: false
lang: en
---

# Scope and version relationship

This supplement accompanies the Strait of Georgia event-study refinement reported in the main manuscript. The refinement is additive and post-result: it does not overwrite, relabel, or supersede the earlier registered analysis of the same linked data, whose tables, figures, locks, hashes, execution record, and manuscript remain unchanged.

The complete aggregate family is reported in `outputs/post_stage4a_sog_event_study_v1/`, with manuscript-ready copies in `tables_v7/`. No protected checklist, observer, locality, source-event identifier, exact coordinate, comment, shoreline field, model object, checkpoint, or record-level derivative is included.

# Event-study estimand

The refinement asks whether the near/reference difference changes through recorded spawning relative to the same spatial difference during days −28 to −15. Near is <5 km from a recorded source point and reference is 5–20 km. Six joint periods are crossed with the two zones. All concurrent source-event links are counted in their observed joint cell, while one checklist remains one model row.

For period *p*, the contrast is:

> (near at period *p* − reference at period *p*) −
> (near at baseline − reference at baseline).

Exponentiated detection values are ratios of near/reference odds ratios. Exponentiated conditional-count values are ratios of near/reference geometric-mean positive-count ratios. Values above one indicate a more positive spatial contrast than baseline; they do not estimate population change.

The primary active contrast is the duration-weighted combination of days 0–3 and 4–14, weighted 4/15 and 11/15 respectively. The pre-spawn summary equally combines days −14 to −8 and −7 to −1, giving the −14 to −1 day window used in the main text and in Figure 3 of the manuscript. The immediate pre-spawn window of days −7 to −1 is reported separately in Table S2. All fixed windows and weights were executed without outcome-selected collapse.

# Taxonomic and count-state rules

Taxa follow eBird/Clements taxonomy v2025. Three taxa in the 49-species family
sit on a taxonomic change within the 2005–2025 window and are resolved to a
single fixed analysis concept by versioned mapping: Short-billed Gull (the former
Mew Gull; common-name and concept change), American Herring Gull (from the split
of the Herring Gull complex), and Iceland Gull (which absorbed Thayer's Gull).
Because the event study includes calendar year as a fixed factor and estimates a
within-year near-versus-reference difference-in-differences, a taxonomy change
that shifts a reported concept equally in both zones within a year is absorbed by
the year factor and differenced out of the interaction; only a within-year,
spatially structured reporting change at a split boundary could bias it, which is
second order.

Each detection carried one explicit count state:

| State | Meaning | Enters detection | Enters count |
|---|---|---|---|
| `numeric` | a finite reported count greater than zero | yes | yes |
| `X` | present but unquantified | yes | no |
| `ambiguity_affected` | identity or count ambiguous under the frozen rules | yes | no |

Lower-bound reports, ambiguous records, structural unknowns, and finite exact
counts remained distinct; none was silently recoded. An unquantified `X` never
became a numeric count, and an ambiguous record was never coerced to a zero
(nondetection). Across the modeled family, `numeric` accounts for the large
majority of detections, `X` for a small fraction that rises with exposure for the
strongest aggregators (main text, Discussion 4.2), and `ambiguity_affected` for a
negligible remainder.

# Complete event-study evidence

The complete family contains 49 core species, both response components where supported, and every registered contrast. Table S1 (`Table_S1_event_study_all_49_species_v7.csv`) retains estimates, standard errors, confidence intervals, ratios, p-values, BH q-values, model sample sizes, status, and multiplicity family for the whole core family.

Table S2 (`Table_S2_main_species_periods_v7.csv`) gives the baseline difference, the 14-day pre-spawn summary, the immediate pre-spawn window, spawn start, early egg, late egg, and the primary active contrast for the eleven species discussed individually in the main text: Surf Scoter, White-winged Scoter, Harlequin Duck, Common Merganser, Hooded Merganser, Glaucous-winged Gull, Short-billed Gull, Bald Eagle, Mallard, American Crow, and Common Raven.

Across estimable core species, BH-adjusted interaction counts were:

| Contrast | Detection positive | Detection negative | Count positive | Count negative |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Pre-spawn, −14 to −1 d | 0 | 0 | 1 | 0 |
| Immediate pre-spawn, −7 to −1 d | 0 | 0 | 2 | 0 |
| Spawn start, 0–3 d | 10 | 4 | 13 | 0 |
| Early egg, 4–14 d | 12 | 2 | 21 | 1 |
| Late egg, 15–28 d | 10 | 2 | 13 | 0 |
| Primary active, 0–14 d | 13 | 6 | 19 | 1 |

Counts refer to BH q < 0.05 within the 49-species outcome-and-contrast family. They are descriptive summaries of correlated contrasts, not independent binomial trials. Detection was estimable for 48 of 49 species and conditional count for 46.

# Family extremes outside the eleven discussed species

The eleven species discussed individually in the main text were named before execution and are not the largest effects in the family. Table 2 of the main manuscript (`Table_2_family_extremes_v7.csv`) lists every BH-significant negative active interaction and every BH-significant positive active interaction outside that panel. The largest positive interactions in the family were American Herring Gull detection 1.49 (1.29–1.71), Iceland Gull detection 1.43 (1.30–1.57), and Long-tailed Duck conditional count 1.49 (1.33–1.67).

Six detection interactions were negative at BH q < 0.05:

| Species | Detection interaction (95% CI) | BH q |
|---|---:|---:|
| Rhinoceros Auklet | 0.71 (0.55–0.91) | 0.0245 |
| Western Grebe | 0.74 (0.60–0.92) | 0.0232 |
| Ring-billed Gull | 0.79 (0.69–0.90) | 0.00159 |
| Black-bellied Plover | 0.84 (0.74–0.95) | 0.0232 |
| Common Loon | 0.90 (0.84–0.97) | 0.0217 |
| Common Raven | 0.94 (0.90–0.98) | 0.0260 |

Ring-billed Gull was the only species negative in both components, with a conditional count interaction of 0.83 (0.74–0.94; q = 0.00956). Its decline is not consistent with a mechanism defined at the level of the gull guild, and it is discussed in the main text on that basis.

# Specificity comparators

Gadwall and Northern Shoveler were detection-only specificity comparators, not guaranteed negative controls. Their complete results are in Table S3 (`Table_S3_specificity_comparators_v7.csv`).

| Species | Baseline near/reference | Pre-spawn | Spawn start | Early egg | Late egg | Active 0–14 d |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Gadwall | 0.88 (0.80–0.97), q = 0.024 | 0.94, q = 0.419 | 1.02, q = 0.856 | 1.03, q = 0.701 | 1.05, q = 0.506 | 1.03 (0.90–1.17), q = 0.704 |
| Northern Shoveler | 1.00 (0.90–1.11), q = 0.949 | 1.07, q = 0.419 | 1.24, q = 0.0917 | 1.25, q = 0.0114 | 1.27, q = 0.00246 | 1.24 (1.08–1.43), q = 0.00564 |

Gadwall did not reproduce the active interaction. Northern Shoveler did, and therefore falsified the third prespecified prediction and demonstrates residual time-varying structure. Candidate explanations include zone-specific migration or habitat use, shoreline access, checklist submission, preferential visitation, source-date or source-point classification, and indirect ecology. The comparator result limits causal and trophic-specific interpretation.

The two nominated comparators understate the issue, because the core family contains further taxa with no established roe-feeding pathway that also rose during days 0–14:

| Species | Outcome | Active 0–14 d (95% CI) | BH q |
|---|---|---:|---:|
| Northern Pintail | detection | 1.29 (1.18–1.42) | 7.32 × 10^-7^ |
| Northern Shoveler (comparator) | detection | 1.24 (1.08–1.43) | 0.00564 |
| American Wigeon | detection | 1.14 (1.07–1.21) | 1.38 × 10^-4^ |
| American Wigeon | positive flock size | 1.11 (1.06–1.16) | 3.51 × 10^-5^ |
| Mallard | positive flock size | 1.11 (1.08–1.14) | 3.69 × 10^-11^ |
| Canada Goose | detection | 1.08 (1.02–1.14) | 0.0206 |

Four of the five dabbling ducks in the study (Mallard, Northern Pintail, American Wigeon, Northern Shoveler) showed a positive active interaction in at least one component; Gadwall was the exception. Northern Pintail's detection interaction exceeded that of every species assigned to the main ecological panel. A two-species comparator panel drawn from a guild that mostly responds cannot bound the false-positive rate, and this is treated as a limitation of the comparator design in the main text rather than as a property of the herring signal.

# Model completion and warnings

The family contained 100 species-response fits and 1,400 contrast rows. Model statuses were 95 completed, one completed with a singular warning, three failed for insufficient support, and one failed numerically without fallback.

| Species | Outcome | Status | Qualification |
|---|---|---|---|
| Surfbird | conditional positive count | failed insufficient support | joint-cell support gate failed |
| Rhinoceros Auklet | conditional positive count | failed insufficient support | joint-cell support gate failed |
| Glaucous Gull | detection | failed numerical fit, no fallback | generic failure code retained; no simplified fit |
| Glaucous Gull | conditional positive count | failed insufficient support | joint-cell support gate failed |
| Western Gull | conditional positive count | completed with singular warning | estimate retained with warning |

All 22 fits for the eleven discussed species completed without convergence failure, rank deficiency, or singularity. The smallest released joint-cell support among them was 259 rows. Global joint support ranged from 2,992 near-zone checklists at spawn start to 28,655 reference-zone checklists in late egg. Exact year counts for near early- and immediate-pre cells were suppressed because fewer than 20 checklist years were represented.

# Numerical, multiplicity, privacy, and integrity checks

All 1,344 finite contrast rows reproduced their exponentiated estimates and confidence limits. No written estimate was `NaN` or infinite. BH q-values were independently recalculated across all 42 role-outcome-contrast families and matched with a maximum absolute difference of 1.78 × 10^-15^.

The production execution used committed code `6bfef2c5b828ca392255d5b3365a2f84b8a2f9f2`. Four protected-input hashes and seven released-output hashes passed. The source-link hash and concurrent-link joint-pairing gates passed. Zero records from 2026 onward were read. The release privacy scan detected no checklist, observer, locality, coordinate, credential, or protected-path violation. Outputs of the earlier registered analysis had no tracked changes.

# Historical registered analyses

The following registered outputs remain part of the complete scientific record and are retained unchanged:

- historical M05 timing and distance family: `tables_v3/Table_S2_all_M05_time_distance_effects_v3.csv`;
- historical M05 guild timing family: `tables_v3/Table_S2a_guild_event_time_effects_v3.csv`;
- historical M08 active-minus-contemporaneous-reference contrasts: `tables_v3/Table_S12_M08_active_minus_reference_v3.csv`;
- historical M02 complete species effects: `tables_v3/Table_S1_all_species_effects_v3.csv`; and
- historical M29 specificity results: `tables_v3/Table_4_specificity_sensitivity_v3.csv`.

M05 used the registered six-window timing structure with early pre-spawn (−42 to −29 d) as baseline and separate additive time and distance margins. M08 was the registered active-minus-reference contrast for the historical active classification. Neither is the present joint period-by-zone difference-in-differences. The historical legacy aggregate release also did not identify the component-level engine after its allowed fitting path. Those facts are preserved rather than repaired retrospectively.

# Reproducibility and governance

Fixture execution tested exact period and zone boundaries, concurrent-link pairing, join cardinality, detection and count semantics, and protected-input guards. Production required the explicit acknowledgement `through_2025_post_result_refinement_v1`. No result was used to change the fixed periods, the primary 0–14 day weights, species definitions, eligibility, model structure, or the specificity panel.

Manuscript figures and tables are rebuilt from the released aggregate outputs by `scripts/build_mer_v7_event_study_assets.py`, which refuses to run unless the execution record reports its review gate and the complete 49-species family is present.

Current results are exploratory and estimand-refining until prospective confirmation. The 2026–2028 response remains locked and may not be inspected through an interim look. A future confirmation must use the complete prospective release and an approved execution package.

# Supplementary file inventory

Every file below is cited in this supplement or in the main manuscript.

| File | Cited as | Content |
|---|---|---|
| `Table_S1_event_study_all_49_species_v7.csv` | Table S1 | complete core-species effect family, all contrasts |
| `Table_S2_main_species_periods_v7.csv` | Table S2 | baseline, pre-spawn, immediate pre-spawn, onset, egg, and active contrasts for the eleven discussed species |
| `Table_S3_specificity_comparators_v7.csv` | Table S3 | Gadwall and Northern Shoveler detection families |
| `Table_1_main_species_active_v7.csv` | Table 1 (main text) | eleven-species primary 0–14 day interactions |
| `Table_2_family_extremes_v7.csv` | Table 2 (main text) | all BH-significant negative interactions and all BH-significant positive interactions outside the eleven |
| `model_diagnostics_v1.csv` | Section 6 | all fit states, warnings, and failures |
| `model_term_support_v1.csv` | Section 6 | joint-cell model support |
| `joint_exposure_support_v1.csv` | Section 6 | global period-by-zone support |
| `execution_record_v1.yml` | Section 7 | authorization, hashes, counts, and final gate |
| `output_hash_manifest_v1.csv` | Section 7 | hashes for released aggregate outputs |
